note: LIVE INCIDENT BANNER updated per CTO decision. Threshold tightened from 150 min to 120 min total elapsed because the chain is only ~12 blocks from the V11 Phase 1 fork at block 7,000 and operational margin to leave the new binary in service is limited if there is any chance it is the cause. Added explicit explanation of what the binary isolation does and does NOT do: it is NOT a chain reset, NOT a block reorg, NOT a UTXO-state change — only the sost-node binary in service on the seed node is swapped to the pre-V11-Phase-2 backup, same chain.json, same blocks, same network. The point is to discard the new binary as a possible cause of unusual behaviour before the chain reaches the V11 Phase 1 hard-fork height. If a block lands quickly with the old binary, the new binary needs investigation before activation. If the chain stays slow with the old binary too, the new binary is exonerated and the cause was hashrate/network slowness. Miner guidance shortened to: chain verified, no fork, block delayed by simultaneous update; keep miners running, do not roll back individually. Probability of >120 min purely from variance at pre-update hashrate ≈ e^(-9.6) ≈ 1 in 14,800. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
